commands.review_summary:
The branch adds media directive parse modes and updates agent, outbound, gateway, and tests so raw tool/output `MEDIA:` text is inert by default while explicit send paths still extract trusted media, plus package/config conflict-resolution hunks.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +225, Config 0, Other -1. Total +232 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the central behavior: the PR body includes terminal output from a local OpenClaw runtime planner showing untrusted raw `MEDIA:` stripped and explicit extraction preserved. The TTS regression is source-reproducible from the PR diff against current-main tests; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is needed because the branch is dirty and changes security-sensitive media trust boundaries; the repairs are clear but should be ported deliberately against current main.

target.title: fix: harden media directive trust boundaries
target.body:
## Summary
- Harden media directive trust boundaries so raw textual `MEDIA:` directives from tool output stay inert.
- Gate local media trust on exact built-in/tool registration provenance and prevent MCP/case-collision/spoofed `trustedLocalMedia` bypasses.
- Strip assistant-visible outbound raw media directives by default while preserving explicit trusted send/gateway `MEDIA:` extraction paths.
- Switch `@openclaw/fs-safe` to npm `0.2.0` because the current GitHub tarball install lacks `dist/` and breaks `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` imports in verification.

## Verification
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD && git diff --check`
- conflict-marker grep on files changed by `HEAD`
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/infra/outbound/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-directives.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts` (13 files, 400 tests)
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- changed-file secret grep, only package/lockfile false positives

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Raw/untrusted textual `MEDIA:` lines must be stripped/inert by default, while trusted explicit send paths can still extract `MEDIA:` attachments.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree at commit `db19194725` on Clawdbot-1, using Node with the runtime outbound payload planner source loaded via `tsx`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
cd /root/clawd/.worktrees/openclaw-media-hardening-phase2-20260507
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import { createOutboundPayloadPlan, projectOutboundPayloadPlanForDelivery, projectOutboundPayloadPlanForMirror } from './src/infra/outbound/payloads.ts';

const untrusted = projectOutboundPayloadPlanForDelivery(
  createOutboundPayloadPlan([{ text: 'Here\nMEDIA:https://example.invalid/should-not-attach.png' }]),
);
const trusted = projectOutboundPayloadPlanForMirror(
  createOutboundPayloadPlan([{ text: 'Here\nMEDIA:https://example.invalid/allowed.png' }], { mediaDirectives: 'extract' }),
);
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  realSetup: 'local OpenClaw worktree command using runtime outbound payload planner',
  defaultUntrustedText: untrusted[0]?.text,
  defaultUntrustedMediaUrls: untrusted[0]?.mediaUrls ?? null,
  trustedSendText: trusted.text,
  trustedSendMediaUrls: trusted.mediaUrls,
}, null, 2));
NODE
```
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the command above:
```text
{
  "realSetup": "local OpenClaw worktree command using runtime outbound payload planner",
  "defaultUntrustedText": "Here",
  "defaultUntrustedMediaUrls": null,
  "trustedSendText": "Here",
  "trustedSendMediaUrls": [
    "https://example.invalid/allowed.png"
  ]
}
```
- Observed result after fix: Default outbound planning stripped the raw untrusted `MEDIA:` directive and produced no media URLs; the explicit trusted extraction path produced a media URL for the intended attachment.
- What was not tested: End-to-end delivery to a live external chat channel from this branch was not sent, to avoid external side effects; local runtime planner behavior and targeted delivery/gateway tests were verified.

## Review
- Independent review completed with verdict: `APPROVED TO PUSH MEDIA HARDENING`.
- CI follow-up review also approved with verdict: `APPROVED TO PUSH MEDIA HARDENING`.
